react.notes: Class lifecycle method deprecated in 16.3; removed in React 19

react.replacement: static getDerivedStateFromProps() or useEffect with deps
react.notes: Class lifecycle method deprecated in 16.3; removed in React 19
Class lifecycle method deprecated in 16.3; removed in React 19
Renamed with UNSAFE_ prefix in 16.3; removed entirely in React 19
Renamed with UNSAFE_ prefix in 16.3; removed entirely in React 19
Renamed with UNSAFE_ prefix in 16.3; removed entirely in React 19
Legacy root API removed in React 19; use createRoot from react-dom/client
Legacy hydration API removed in React 19; use hydrateRoot from react-dom/client
Removed in React 19; use the unmount method on the root returned by createRoot
Removed in React 19; use refs instead
Removed in React 19; use JSX or createElement
Deprecated for function components in 18.3; removed in React 19. Class components still support defaultProps
Moved to separate prop-types package in 15.5; propTypes silently ignored in React 19
String refs removed in React 19; use createRef, useRef, or ref callbacks
Legacy context API removed in React 19; use createContext and useContext

react.replacement: ref is a regular prop in React 19+
react.notes: In React 19, ref is passed as a regular prop; forwardRef is no longer needed but still works

react.replacement: act() moved to react package; use @testing-library/react
react.notes: All react-dom/test-utils functions removed in React 19; act moved to react package
Shallow renderer removed in React 19; was already a re-export of react-shallow-renderer since 18
UMD builds removed in React 19; use ESM-based CDN for script tag usage
Anti-pattern; React.Children utilities are fragile with fragments and keys

nextjs.notes: Pages Router pattern; in App Router, fetch data directly in server components. Still works in Pages Router

nextjs.replacement: Server Components with fetch and caching
nextjs.notes: Pages Router pattern; in App Router, use fetch with cache options
Pages Router pattern; in App Router, export generateStaticParams
Legacy data fetching; avoid in both Pages and App Router

nextjs.replacement: Metadata API (export const metadata or generateMetadata)
nextjs.notes: In App Router, use the Metadata API instead of next/head
Pages Router file; in App Router, use layout.tsx
Pages Router file; in App Router, put html/body in root layout
In App Router, import from next/navigation not next/router

In Next.js 16, middleware.ts is renamed to proxy.ts. Export a proxy function. Runs on Node.js runtime only (Edge runtime not supported in proxy). Configuration flags like skipMiddlewareUrlNormalize renamed to skipProxyUrlNormalize

nextjs.deprecated: Synchronous access to cookies(), headers(), draftMode(), params, searchParams
nextjs.replacement: await cookies(), await headers(), await params, etc.
nextjs.notes: Async request APIs introduced in Next.js 15 with sync compatibility. In Next.js 16, synchronous access is fully removed; must use await or React.use() in client components
TypeScript config files preferred since Next.js 15; .js still works but .ts is recommended
The next export command is removed; use output: 'export' in next.config
The layout prop is removed; use fill for layout=fill, or width+height
Use standard CSS style prop instead of dedicated props
Since Next.js 13, Link renders an anchor tag; no child <a> needed
In App Router, use route.ts with GET/POST exports instead of pages/api
Route Handlers use Web API Request; no req.query available
Route Handlers return Response objects instead of using res methods

nextjs.replacement: App Router is stable and enabled by default
nextjs.notes: The appDir flag was removed; App Router is default since Next.js 13.4
Legacy image component deprecated in 13 and removed in Next.js 16

nextjs.replacement: No replacement; AMP adoption has declined
nextjs.notes: All AMP APIs and configurations removed in Next.js 16
next lint command removed in Next.js 16; next build no longer runs linting
Runtime configs removed in Next.js 16; use .env files instead
PPR evolved into Cache Components in Next.js 16; use 'use cache' directive instead of global ppr flag
ISR revalidation is stable; drop the unstable_ prefix

nodejs.notes: Security issue: Buffer() and new Buffer() are unsafe; use static methods
Legacy URL API; use the WHATWG URL API (new URL()). Runtime deprecated in Node.js 24
Legacy URL API; use the WHATWG URL constructor
Legacy URL API; construct a URL object and call toString()
Considered legacy but NOT formally deprecated. URLSearchParams is preferred for new code but querystring still works and has no deprecation warning
Callback has non-standard signature; use fs.access or fs.stat. Note: fs.existsSync() is NOT deprecated
Domain module is deprecated; use AsyncLocalStorage for context propagation

nodejs.replacement: npm install punycode or use URL API
nodejs.notes: Deprecated in Node.js core; install from npm if needed
createCipher derives key without salt; use createCipheriv with explicit IV
Same issue as createCipher; use createDecipheriv
Property access deprecated; use getter/setter methods

nodejs.replacement: No direct replacement; use public APIs
nodejs.notes: Internal binding layer; not meant for public use
Use Buffer.allocUnsafeSlow() for non-pooled buffers
Use tls.TLSSocket directly. Removed in Node.js 24
Inconsistent casing was fixed; use lowercase tmpdir()
Legacy util output functions; use console methods
Use stream.pipeline for proper error handling
Type-checking functions deprecated; use native JS operators

nodejs.replacement: console.log() with timestamp or a logging library
nodejs.notes: Deprecated; use a proper logging solution
Internal utility; use Object.assign or { ...obj }
The assert keyword for import assertions is replaced by with keyword (import attributes) in Node.js 22+. Old syntax removed in Node.js 22
sys was renamed to util very early in Node.js history

express.notes: Renamed from del to delete; removed in Express 5
Removed in Express 3; just call express()
Built-in bodyParser removed in Express 4; use express.json() and express.urlencoded() since 4.16
Removed from Express core; install cookie-parser separately
Removed from Express core; install express-session separately
Removed from Express core; install method-override separately
Removed from Express core; install morgan separately
Removed from Express core; install serve-favicon separately
Removed from Express core; install compression separately
Removed from Express core; write custom error middleware
Removed from Express core; install serve-index separately
Removed from Express core; install multer for file uploads
Lowercase sendfile deprecated in 4.8; removed in Express 5
Passing status as first arg removed in Express 5; chain status().json()
Passing status as first arg removed in Express 5; chain status().send()

express.deprecated: res.send(status) where status is a number
express.notes: Sending a bare number removed in Express 5; use sendStatus or status().send()
Passing status as second arg to jsonp removed in Express 5
Magic string 'back' removed in Express 5; use req.get('Referrer') manually
Searched params/body/query which is ambiguous; removed in Express 5

express.replacement: Use app.param(name, fn) with standard signature
express.notes: The app.param(fn) callback signature removed in Express 5
Removed in Express 4; use plain conditionals
Singular forms removed in Express 5; use plural forms

express.replacement: Use simpler path patterns or named params
express.notes: Express 5 changed path-to-regexp to avoid ReDoS attacks; sub-expressions no longer supported

typescript.notes: Replaced by verbatimModuleSyntax which is more predictable
Was a compatibility flag for pre-2.9 behavior; no longer relevant
Use noUncheckedIndexedAccess for safer index access

typescript.replacement: No replacement; fix the excess properties
typescript.notes: Suppressing type errors is not recommended
Disabling strict checks is not recommended
Renamed from out to outFile for clarity

typescript.replacement: No replacement needed; UTF-8 is default
typescript.notes: TypeScript only supports UTF-8; this option had no effect
ES3 target deprecated in TS 5.0; ES5 also deprecated in TS 6.0. Minimum target is now ES2015
ES5 target deprecated in TypeScript 6.0; lowest target is now ES2015. Will be removed in TS 7.0
AMD, UMD, and SystemJS module systems deprecated in TS 6.0; will be removed in TS 7.0

typescript.replacement: No replacement; only applied to ES5 emit which is now deprecated
typescript.notes: Since target ES5 is deprecated, downlevelIteration has no purpose. Deprecated in TS 6.0

typescript.replacement: Use paths with explicit mappings or remove baseUrl
typescript.notes: baseUrl deprecated in TS 6.0 when not used with paths; rootDir now defaults to config directory. Will stop functioning in TS 7.0
Triple-slash references are legacy; use module imports
Namespaces are legacy; use ES module syntax for code organization
The 'node' resolution mode doesn't support exports/imports in package.json
The react mode requires React in scope; react-jsx uses the new JSX transform
TypeScript 5.8 errors on import assertions under --module nodenext; use 'with' keyword instead of 'assert'
Not officially deprecated but widely discouraged; use as const for union types

python.replacement: list[], dict[], tuple[], set[], frozenset[] (built-in generics)
python.notes: PEP 585: built-in types support subscripting since 3.9; typing aliases deprecated. Scheduled for removal after Python 3.9 EOL (Oct 2025)
PEP 604 introduced X | None syntax in 3.10; Optional[X] still works but is considered legacy
PEP 604 introduced X | Y syntax in 3.10; Union still works but is considered legacy
Use collections.abc.Callable for type hints since 3.9
Use the actual collection classes with subscripting since 3.9
Use re module types directly since 3.9

python.replacement: Import from io and re directly
python.notes: typing.io and typing.re namespaces removed in Python 3.13
Generator-based coroutines deprecated in 3.8; removed in 3.11. Use async def
Generator-based coroutine syntax removed in 3.11; use await

python.deprecated: Explicit loop parameter in asyncio functions
python.replacement: Omit loop parameter (uses running loop automatically)
python.notes: Removed in 3.10 from asyncio.sleep, gather, shield, wait_for, wait, Lock, Event, Semaphore, Queue, etc.
Raises DeprecationWarning in 3.10+ when no running loop; raises RuntimeError in 3.14+. Use asyncio.run() as entry point
Deprecated in 3.12; will be removed in 3.16. Use inspect.iscoroutinefunction()
Dead batteries: removed in Python 3.13 along with 17 other legacy stdlib modules

python.deprecated: aifc, audioop, chunk, crypt, imghdr, mailcap, msilib, nis, nntplib, ossaudiodev, pipes, sndhdr, spwd, sunau, telnetlib, uu, xdrlib
python.notes: PEP 594 dead batteries: 19 legacy modules deprecated in 3.11 and removed in 3.13

python.replacement: No replacement needed (Python 2 migration is complete)
python.notes: Removed in Python 3.13; Python 2 migration tooling no longer maintained
Removed in Python 3.13; use tkinter.ttk widgets instead
Removed in Python 3.12; use setuptools for building and packaging for metadata
Deprecated in 3.11 and removed in 3.13; use TestLoader directly

go.notes: ioutil.ReadAll -> io.ReadAll, ioutil.ReadFile -> os.ReadFile, ioutil.WriteFile -> os.WriteFile, ioutil.TempDir -> os.MkdirTemp, ioutil.TempFile -> os.CreateTemp, ioutil.ReadDir -> os.ReadDir, ioutil.NopCloser -> io.NopCloser, ioutil.Discard -> io.Discard
The context package was moved to stdlib in Go 1.7; x/net/context is a shim that just re-exports it
errors.Is, errors.As, errors.Unwrap, fmt.Errorf with %w added in Go 1.13; xerrors is deprecated
Global Seed deprecated in Go 1.20. For reproducible sequences use rand.New(). For general random, use math/rand/v2
math/rand.Read deprecated; use crypto/rand for cryptographic randomness
math/rand/v2 introduced in Go 1.22 with better APIs and generators (PCG, ChaCha8). Prefer v2 for new code
Experimental rand package deprecated in favor of stdlib math/rand/v2

go.deprecated: errors.As with manual type assertion pattern
go.notes: errors.AsType provides type-safe alternative to errors.As without manual var declaration. go fix can auto-update
slices.Sort and slices.SortFunc introduced in 1.21 are simpler and faster; sort.Sort still works but is legacy
any is an alias for interface{} since Go 1.18; any is preferred in new code
go get no longer builds packages since Go 1.18; use go install for executables
GOPATH mode deprecated since modules introduced in 1.11; module mode is default since 1.16; GOPATH mode removed in 1.17

css_tailwind.notes: Tailwind v4 moves all configuration to CSS using @theme directives. JS config files no longer required
Tailwind v4 uses native CSS cascade layers; custom utility classes must use the @utility API

css_tailwind.deprecated: bg-opacity-*, text-opacity-*, border-opacity-*, ring-opacity-*, divide-opacity-*, placeholder-opacity-*
css_tailwind.notes: All separate opacity utilities removed in v4; use color/opacity syntax
Gradient classes renamed for consistency; deprecated in 4.0, removed in 4.1
Shorthand aliases added in v3; old long names removed in v4.1
Renamed for clarity; removed in v4.1
Renamed to match CSS property name
Important modifier moved from prefix to suffix in v4; old syntax still supported but deprecated
Renamed for clarity; outline-none now maps to outline-style: none in v4
Size scale shifted in v4: xs was added, all sizes renamed down. Use npx @tailwindcss/upgrade to auto-convert
Most vendor prefixes unnecessary in modern browsers. Use Autoprefixer/PostCSS if needed. Only -webkit- still needed for a few Safari properties
Not formally deprecated in CSS spec but float-based layouts are a legacy pattern. Use flex/grid

prisma.notes: Client middleware deprecated in 4.16; removed in 6.14. Use Client Extensions ($extends) for query interception
rejectOnNotFound removed in Prisma 5; use OrThrow variants instead
NotFoundError removed in Prisma 6; findUniqueOrThrow and findFirstOrThrow now throw PrismaClientKnownRequestError with P2025
Prisma 6 uses Uint8Array instead of Buffer for Bytes fields
Runtime imports deprecated in 4.10; import from @prisma/client instead
Prisma 7 requires a driver adapter for all databases, making the client more lean and modular
fullTextSearch is GA for MySQL only; PostgreSQL users must use fullTextSearchPostgres preview feature
Not formally deprecated but db push is for prototyping only; use migrate deploy in production

zod.deprecated: z.string().email(), z.string().uuid(), z.string().url() (method chaining for formats)
zod.notes: String format validators moved from methods to top-level functions in Zod 4. Method forms deprecated

zod.deprecated: z.string().emoji(), z.string().base64(), z.string().nanoid(), z.string().cuid(), z.string().cuid2(), z.string().ulid()
zod.replacement: z.emoji(), z.base64(), z.nanoid(), z.cuid(), z.cuid2(), z.ulid()
zod.notes: All string format validators moved to top-level in Zod 4
IP and CIDR validators moved to top-level in Zod 4
Zod 4 standardizes on 'error' param for error customization. 'message' still works but is deprecated
Instance method deprecated in favor of top-level z.treeifyError()
Instance method deprecated in favor of top-level z.treeifyError()
Methods still exist for backward compatibility but top-level functions are preferred in Zod 4

zod.replacement: z.guid() for v3-compatible behavior, z.uuid() for RFC 4122 strict
zod.notes: Zod 4 z.uuid() enforces strict RFC 4122; v3's z.string().uuid() was looser. Use z.guid() for backward compatibility
z.promise() removed in Zod 4; await values before passing to schema validation

authjs_nextauth.notes: In Auth.js v5, import from 'next-auth' directly, not 'next-auth/next'
Middleware export is now from the main next-auth package
NEXTAUTH_ prefix replaced with AUTH_ prefix. AUTH_URL is not strictly necessary in most environments
All NEXTAUTH_ env vars should be renamed to AUTH_ prefix
In v5, a single auth() function replaces getServerSession for all server-side auth checks
Prefer useSession() in client components or auth() in server components

authjs_nextauth.replacement: Named GET/POST exports from auth handler
authjs_nextauth.notes: v5 uses named exports: export const { GET, POST } = handlers where handlers comes from NextAuth config
OAuth 1.0 support removed in v5. Remove oauth_token_secret and oauth_token from account table
Cookie prefix changed from next-auth to authjs. Existing sessions break on upgrade; plan migration carefully

authjs_nextauth.replacement: JWT with salt in key derivation
authjs_nextauth.notes: v5 uses salt in JWT encryption by default, changing the key derivation process from v4. Tokens are incompatible across versions
v5 built on @auth/core with stricter OAuth/OIDC compliance. Minimum Next.js 14.0 required
